title: Python Rest api
content:
Part 1: Flask Philosophy & The "Why" Behind the Boilerplate
Flask ko "Micro-framework" kaha jata hai. Lekin interview mein agar pucha jaye ki "Micro" ka matlab kya hai, toh log galti karte hain. "Micro" ka matlab "chota" nahi, balki "Extensible" hota hai. Flask aapko basic tools deta hai, aur baaki cheezein (Database, Auth) aap apni marzi se choose karte ho.
1. Initializing the Engine
Sabse pehle yeh basic code dekho:
from flask import Flask
app = Flask(__name__)
  • Deep Concept: app = Flask(__name__) sirf ek boilerplate line nahi hai. __name__ Flask ko batata hai ki app ka root path kya hai. Isse Flask ko pata chalta hai ki templates aur static files kahan dhundni hain.
  • ise flask app initialise hota h 
2. The Decorator Pattern (@app.route)
@app.route('/')
def hello_world():
    return 'Hello, World!'
  • Interview Focus (Odoo/Target Integration): Odoo mein bhi aap @http.route use karte hain. Flask mein @app.route('/') ek Decorator hai. Yeh Flask ke "Routing Map" mein ek entry register karta hai ki jab koi "/" hit kare, toh hello_world function execute ho.

how to run the code
code ko chlane ke liye yeh
jrur likho 


if __name__ == "__main__":    app.run(debug=True)


if __name__ == "__main__":
app.run(debug=True)

Haan, app = Flask("my_app") likhoge toh bhi code chalega ✅

app = Flask("my_app")

Flask ko bas ek application name chahiye hota hai.
Lekin generally hum:

app = Flask(__name__)

isliye use karte hain kyunki __name__ dynamic hota hai aur Flask ko app ka correct location/context milta hai.

__name__ se kya hota h kki iseka mtlb hota h flask ko btan ki hum konsi file use kr rhe h or uska naam kya h agat hi,m __name__ ki jgh naam bhi likhenge toh voh bhi chl jaayega 

__name__ ka actual fayda

Python me __name__ ek special variable hota hai.

Case 1: File direct run karo

Agar:

python main.py

toh __name__ ki value hoti hai:

"__main__"

Isliye ye condition true ho jaati hai:

if __name__ == "__main__":

aur app.run() execute hota hai.


______________________________________________________________________________________________________
Part 2: Modular Architecture & MVC Pattern (Odoo Style Scaling)
Real-world projects (aur khaas kar Odoo) mein kabhi bhi saara code ek hi file (app.py) mein nahi likha jata. Agar tum 100 routes ek hi file mein likh doge, toh debugging ek nightmare ban jayegi.


1. MVC (Model-View-Controller) Architecture
Hum yahan MVC architecture follow karenge taaki code maintainable rahe:
  • Model: Yeh data aur database logic handle karta hai (Database ke saath khelta hai).
  • Controller: Yeh request receive karta hai aur Model ko kaam batata hai (Endpoints ke saath khelta hai).
  • View: Hum REST API bana rahe hain, isliye humara "View" sirf JSON response hoga.


2. Multi-File Architecture Mein Todna
Jab hum code ko alag files mein todte hain, toh ek bada challenge aata hai: Flask ko kaise pata chalega ki dusri files mein bhi routes hain?.
  • Flask by default sirf app.py ko load karta hai.
  • Agar tumne user_controller.py banaya hai, toh tumhe use app.py mein import karna padega taaki Flask ke routing map mein wo register ho sake.

3. The __init__.py Magic
Jab tum controllers ko ek folder (controller/) mein daalte ho, toh Python ko batana padta hai ki yeh ek Package hai.
  • __init__.py file folder ke andar hone se Python use package ki tarah treat karta hai.
  • Deep Concept: Odoo mein bhi har module ke andar ek __init__.py hota hai jo baki sub-folders ko import karta hai. Bina iske Odoo ko pata hi nahi chalega ki aapne koi naya model ya controller banaya hai.


💡 Interview Focus: Target Integration / Odoo
Question: "Agar hum app.py mein controllers ko import na karein, toh kya hoga?" Answer: Flask application run toh ho jayegi, lekin jab tum us controller wale URL ko hit karoge, toh 404 Not Found milega. Kyunki Flask ne kabhi us file ko scan hi nahi kiya, toh use pata hi nahi chala ki wahan koi @app.route define kiya gaya hai.

Example:

return jsonify(result)

Ye browser/client ko batata hai ki response JSON format me hai.

withou jsonify flask automaticalay conver tbhi kar deta h but reliable nhi hota 

🔸 1. Flask Kya Hai? Flask ek lightweight Python library hai jo web apps aur REST APIs banane ke liye use hoti hai. 4 python REST API Iska syntax simple hai, isiliye beginners ke liye perfect hai. Flask tumhe allow karta hai ki tum easily routes URLs aur endpoints define kar sako

from flask import Flask app = Flask(__name__) # Yeh Flask app object ban gaya Flask → ek class hai, jisme bahut saare built-in tools hote hain (like routing, request handling, etc). __name__ → yeh current file ka naam batata hai, Flask ko yeh batata hai ki kaha se start karna hai.

@app.route("/") → yeh ek decorator hai, jo Flask ko batata hai ki jab koi user / URL hit kare, toh neeche wala function chale. Function ka naam kuch bhi ho sakta hai, yeh user-defined hota hai. Is example mein hello() function HTML return kar raha hai.
flask run
naam kuch aur ho toh: $env:FLASK_APP="myfile.py"
$env:FLASK_ENV="development"

Yeh mode auto-reload ko enable karta hai – jaise hi tu code save karega, Flask auto refresh ho jayega. Plus, agar koi error aata hai, toh detailed error page bhi dikhata hai

bash  PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
� Multi-File Architecture
( MVC Model-View-Controller)

MODEL - database ke sath khelta hai 
Controller - route ya endpoint ke sath khelta h 
view - view webpage dikhata hai 

❌ Problem Kya Aayi?
Agar dusri file mein koi route banaya toh woh trigger nahi hota, kyunki Flask us file ko directly nahi jaanta. pdega
chlta toh app.py h phle toh baaki ki things kese chlegi 

user_controller.py
from flask import request
from app import app  # Yeh import jaruri hai
@app.route("/user")
def get_user():
  return {"msg": "User controller se response aaya"}

⚠ from app import app ka matlab: app.py wale app instance ko yahan use karna 


from flask import Flask
app = Flask(__name__)
# Yeh line app initialize hone ke baad hi honi chahiye
import user_controller


🔁 Yeh import karna zaroori hai warna Flask ko user_controller.py ke route ka pata nahi chalega.

🔄 Extra Tip: Yeh Architecture Kaise Help Karega?

  • Modularization: Tum apna code cleanly tod paoge jaise product_controller.py, order_controller.py, etc.
  • Scalability: Jab project bada hoga, toh sab kuch ek file mein manage karna mushkil ho jaata hai.
  • Maintainability: Bugs aur features alag-alag modules mein easily trace ho jaate hain.

✅ Step 2: app.py Mein Import Kaise Karein? 

from controller import user_controller, product_controller


🟢 Works fine, lekin agar 10-15 controller ho gaye toh har baar manually likhna padta hai.


Ab socha ki * (wildcard import) use kar lein

yeh directly work nhi krta python ko nahi pata hota ki folder ek package hai ya nahi 


✅ Solution: __init__.py Se Package Banana

Jab tu controller/ folder ke andar init.py file bana deta hai, toh Python ko signal milta hai:

"Bhai yeh ek package hai, isko import kar sakte ho!" sirf package ko hi import kar skt eho


toh hume all ke andar likhni pdti h saari file but what if multiple files h toh itni saari chije all me kese likhoge

toh ek shortcut h ki jisme init.py chodke saari .py all me likhdiye jaayenge


__all__ = [f.replace(".py", "") for f in os.listdir(os.path.dirname(__file__)) if f.endswith(".py") and f != "__init__.py"]


🔥 Yeh code automatically sabhi python files ko __all__ list mein daal deta hai. Bas, ab from controller import * likho — and done ✅ but ek problem h yeh sirf locally hi chelga bina local envionmnet ke yeh nhi chelga kyuki yeh operating system ki tasks 

from controller import *


  • ab chl jaayega yeh phle directl chla rhe the tab nhi chl rha tha ;

NOW EK OR FOLDER banyange hum 
user_model.py ke naam se
class user_Model() : 
 def user_signup_model(self):  
  return "this is working and user signup model" 

uske andar yeh sab likh rhe honge 

model kya hota h database se khleta h controller route se 


controller ke andar ise import karenge 

from model.user_model import user_Model



obj = user_Model()

uska  object bhi leke aayenge 

Ab us object ke through hum model ka method call karenge: python Copy code return obj.user_signup_model()

ab humne work bench me jaake **table** banali hai 
and iska ab connect krenge 
MySQL ek server hai jahan humara data store hota hai. 
Workbench ek GUI tool hai jo hum jaise users ke liye use hota hai – taaki hum easily queries run kar sakein. 
Ab hum Python ke through bhi MySQL se directly connect karenge – bina workbench ke. 🛠 Python ko MySQL se Conn
MySQL server installed hona chahiye (jo already hai). 


MySQL server installed hona chahiye (jo already hai).
pip install mysql-connector-python

u ser_model.py mein Import karna

import mysql.connector


AB hum code bnayenge jo baar baar connection ka code repeat na kare ;

isliye hum ek constructor __Init__ bnayenge class ke andar 


jab bhi kis class ka object banate ho , toh __init__ chkegeaa 

isse kya hoga jese user_Model ka object banega waise hi mysql ke sath connection establish ho jaye 


mysql.connector kya hai?

Ye ek Python library hai jo:

  • MySQL database se connection banati hai
  • SQL queries execute karwati hai
  • Data insert/update/delete/fetch karti hai
  • Transactions handle karti hai


mysql.connector kya hai?

Ye ek Python library hai jo:

  • MySQL database se connection banati hai
  • SQL queries execute karwati hai
  • Data insert/update/delete/fetch karti hai
  • Transactions handle karti hai

3. Basic Syntax

mysql.connector.connect(    host="localhost",    user="root",    password="root",    database="mydb")

Example:

mysql.connector.connect(  
  host="localhost",  
  user="root",    
password="root",  
  port=3306)


ArgumentPurposeExampleDefault
hostMySQL server ka address"localhost""127.0.0.1"
userMySQL username"root"None
passwordUser ka password"root"None
databaseKaunsa database use karna hai"flask_tutorial"None
portMySQL server port33063306
auth_pluginAuthentication method"mysql_native_password"Auto
charsetCharacter encoding"utf8""utf8"
autocommitAuto save changesTrueFalse
connection_timeoutConnection wait time (seconds)10System default
use_unicodeUnicode supportTrueTrue
ssl_disabledSSL disable karnaTrueFalse
bufferedCursor result memory me storeTrueFalse
pool_nameConnection pool ka naam"mypool"None
pool_sizePool me connections count55
raise_on_warningsSQL warnings ko exception bananaTrueFalse
consume_resultsPending results auto consumeTrueFalse
